title: Model Evaluation Funnel Follow-Up
goal: Address remaining model-evaluation findings by strengthening the package route for durable state and planning-only broad preparation.
non_goals: Do not redesign the full Planning workflow or add host-specific CI/PR policy.
Do not treat evaluator scoring alone as the product fix.

intent.outcome: Make agents more likely to create canonical durable planning state for broad preparation instead of proposal-only answers, WORKFLOW.md edits, root handoff files, or product scaffolds.
intent.constraints: Keep changes bounded to startup/planning routing surfaces, generated fixtures, evaluator scoring, and this execplan.
intent.latitude: Tighten CLI payloads, workflow wording, planning projections, tests, and model-evaluation scenarios.
intent.escalation: Escalate when a better-looking fix changes the requested outcome, owned surface, time horizon, or meaningful validation story.

execution.next_step: Tighten planning-only and durable-state bridge routes, then validate with focused tests, make check, and targeted dynamic evaluations.
execution.proof: Focused tests, make check, and refreshed dynamic evaluation of the affected scenarios.

invariants: Preserve the planning contract and keep the work bounded to this plan.
Keep host repo agent instructions as a thin managed fence.
Planning-only preparation must route to canonical planning/decomposition state and must not authorize product scaffolding.

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: Improve package robustness when weaker external agents are asked to prepare broad work without explicit package steering.

iterative_follow_through.next likely slice: continue the current milestone until the completion criteria are met

intent_interpretation.literal request: Address your findings from the evaluation
intent_interpretation.inferred intended outcome: Implement the remaining package-side fixes from dynamic model evaluation, especially durable-state routing and planning-only broad prep.
intent_interpretation.chosen concrete what: Strengthen CLI/workflow guidance and evaluator coverage for canonical prep-only routes.
intent_interpretation.review guidance: Confirm the scaffolded plan still matches the promoted item before broad implementation.

execution_bounds.allowed paths: Startup pointer/config payloads, planning installer/checker, model CLI harness suite/tests, root AGENTS.md, and this execplan.
execution_bounds.max changed files: About 20 files; stop before touching generated package internals unrelated to startup/planning routing.
execution_bounds.required validation commands: uv run pytest focused tests for planning help/summary/checker/model harness; make check; targeted model CLI scenarios if tool availability permits.
execution_bounds.ask-before-refactor threshold: Ask before adding a new major command surface or changing planning schema compatibility.
execution_bounds.stop before touching: Unrelated backlog, adjacent modules, or canonical contracts not named by this plan.

stop_conditions.stop when: The work no longer matches the promoted item or its completion criteria.
stop_conditions.escalate when boundary reached: A correct fix requires changing the requested outcome or ownership boundary.
stop_conditions.escalate on scope drift: Implementation needs files outside the filled execution bounds.
stop_conditions.escalate on proof failure: The selected proof cannot demonstrate the completion criteria.

context_budget.live working set: This execplan, the promoted item, and the narrow files needed for the current implementation step.
context_budget.recoverable later: Repo background, historical reviews, and deferred backlog unless compact outputs point there.
context_budget.externalize before shift: Update execution_run, proof_report, finished_run_review, and closeout_distillation before pausing.
context_budget.pre-work config pull: Use compact config/startup/summary outputs before opening raw planning or routing files.
context_budget.pre-work memory pull: Route to the narrowest relevant memory only when the task needs durable repo knowledge.
context_budget.tiny resumability note: Fill in execution bounds, touched paths, and validation before implementation starts.
context_budget.context-shift triggers: Proof failure, scope drift, interruption, handoff, or closeout.

delegated_judgment.requested outcome: Create a bounded plan for Model Evaluation Funnel Follow-Up.
delegated_judgment.hard constraints: Keep scope bounded to the promoted TODO item and its stated touched paths.
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: Bounded decomposition, touched-path narrowing, validation tightening, and plan-local residue routing.
delegated_judgment.escalate when: A better-looking fix changes the requested outcome, owned surface, time horizon, or meaningful validation story.

system_intent_alignment.relevant system intent: Preserve the larger intended outcome separately from this bounded slice.
system_intent_alignment.slice shaping bias: Keep the slice bounded while carrying any larger follow-on through explicit continuation fields.
system_intent_alignment.broader-lane validation question: Did this slice advance the declared larger outcome, or only complete the local task?

active_milestone.scope: Keep this execution thread bounded to the promoted TODO item.

immediate_next_action: Tighten planning-only and durable-state bridge routes, then validate with focused tests, make check, and targeted dynamic evaluations.
invariants: Preserve the planning contract and keep the work bounded to this plan.
validation_commands: uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_cli.py tests/test_model_cli_harness.py packages/planning/tests/test_check_planning_surfaces.py packages/planning/tests/test_installer.py
targeted model CLI harness scenarios for broad-work-decomposition and native-plan-bridge
completion_criteria: Model Evaluation Funnel Follow-Up is implemented, validated, and closed out honestly.

execution_run.what happened: Strengthened prep-only and durable-state routing, added a prep-only scaffold mode, projected prep-only halt state in summary, detected non-planning drift under active prep-only plans, and tightened dynamic model evaluation scoring.
execution_run.scope touched: startup workflow payloads, planning CLI/installer/checker, model CLI harness, tests, root AGENTS.md, and this execplan
execution_run.changed surfaces: AGENTS.md, .agentic-workspace/WORKFLOW.md, src/agentic_workspace/cli.py, planning package CLI/installer/checker/tests, model CLI harness tests/fixtures
execution_run.validations run: Focused pytest for planning CLI/help/harness; make check passed after rerun; dynamic broad-work evaluations for Codex Spark, Copilot Haiku, and Gemini Flash.
execution_run.result for continuation: No immediate continuation required; #684 and #685 were closed with evidence.

finished_run_review.config compliance: used checked-in planning, package commands, dogfooding evaluation, and issue closeout
finished_run_review.misinterpretation risk: Residual weak-agent obedience risk remains, but package summary and harness now detect the drift.

proof_report.validation proof: uv run pytest focused planning/harness tests; make check; dynamic model CLI broad-work evaluations.
proof_report.evidence for \"proof achieved\" state: make check passed. Dynamic evaluation: Codex Spark and Gemini Flash stayed within Planning surfaces; Copilot/Haiku still edited README.md, now detected by harness and by prep_only_scope_violation when summary is rerun.

intent_satisfaction.original intent: Address model-evaluation findings around durable planning routes and planning-only broad prep.
intent_satisfaction.evidence of intent satisfaction: Implemented package guidance, structured --prep-only scaffolding, summary projection/detection, evaluator scoring, tests, and issue closeout.

execution_summary.outcome delivered: Prep-only planning handoff is now CLI-addressable, machine-readable, summary-visible, and checked against non-planning drift.
execution_summary.validation confirmed: make check passed and targeted model CLI evaluation was rerun.
execution_summary.post-work posterity capture: Issues #684 and #685 were updated and closed.
execution_summary.knowledge promoted (Memory/Docs/Config): workflow payloads, planning CLI output, and tests

closure_check.why this decision is honest: The package cannot force an external agent to obey prose, but it now provides a structured prep-only command, machine-readable halt state, summary drift detection, and harness scoring for the observed failure.
closure_check.evidence carried forward: make check passed; dynamic evaluation transcripts under scratch/model-cli-harness; GitHub issues #684 and #685 closed.
closure_check.reopen trigger: A future evaluation shows prep-only drift that is not visible in summary, proof, report, or harness scoring.

improvement_signal_review.guidance: At closeout, separate acted-on, reported-only/routed, and dismissed incidental findings; keep this compact and evidence-backed.
signals found: Copilot/Haiku interpreted prep-only broad work as project setup even after reading workflow prose.
The harness missed modified README.md as a prep-only semantic failure.
signals fixed: Added --prep-only scaffold mode with halt contract.
Added prep_only_scope_violation for non-planning git changes under an active prep-only plan.
Updated harness scoring to include modified non-planning files.

drift_log: 2026-05-02: Completed prep-only routing and evaluation follow-up; ready to archive.
